options: Forces all files to use TypeScript
Makes .js files treated as ES modules
Enables Node.js type checking
Loads the types field from package exports
questions.explanation: "type": "module" tells Node.js to treat .js files as ESM. Without it, .js files default to CommonJS.

questions.prompt: A CommonJS module needs to use a pure ESM package. What is the correct approach?
options: Add @ts-ignore at the top
Use require() — it automatically handles ESM
Use dynamic import(): const mod = await import('pkg')
Downgrade the package to its CommonJS version
questions.explanation: Dynamic import() works in CJS and returns a Promise. It is the only way to load a pure ESM module from CJS.

questions.prompt: What is the most critical tsconfig setting for TypeScript strictness?
options: noImplicitAny
strict
noUncheckedIndexedAccess
strictPropertyInitialization
questions.explanation: strict: true enables all strict checks at once. It's the master flag — don't enable them piecemeal.

questions.prompt: When should you set noEmit: true in tsconfig?
options: For library packages
When you only want to generate .d.ts files
When a bundler handles transpilation and tsc is only for type checking
For test files only
questions.explanation: When Vite, Webpack, or esbuild compiles TypeScript, tsc's job is type checking only. noEmit prevents it from generating JS files.

questions.prompt: ESM has tree-shaking advantages over CJS because:
options: ESM modules are loaded synchronously
ESM imports/exports are static and analyzable at build time
CJS is not supported by modern bundlers
ESM modules are smaller by default
questions.explanation: ESM imports are static — the shape of exports is known at parse time. Bundlers can determine which exports are unused and eliminate them.

questions.prompt: With module: NodeNext, what extension must you use in import statements for TypeScript files?
questions.answer_key: .js — e.g., import { foo } from './bar.js'. TypeScript resolves bar.js to bar.ts. This mirrors Node.js ESM's requirement for explicit extensions.
